holdings.what_it_does: Creates the flavours and fragrances used inside everyday food, drinks, perfumes, and beauty products — the company is the invisible ingredient behind many of the world's best-known brands.
holdings.dividend_yield_pct: ~1.8% (CHF 72.00 per share proposed for FY2025, 25th consecutive annual increase)
why_own_it: Near-monopoly position: Givaudan and one rival control the majority of the global flavours and fragrances market, making it very hard for new competitors to take their place.
Sticky, repeat business: food and beverage companies rarely switch flavour suppliers because reformulating a product is costly and risky — so Givaudan keeps its customers for years.
25 years of unbroken dividend growth: the company has raised its dividend every single year since listing on the Swiss stock exchange in 2000, rewarding patient shareholders.
holdings.one_thing_to_watch: A rising Swiss franc hurts Givaudan's reported revenues because most of its sales are earned in foreign currencies (euros, dollars, etc.) that shrink in value when converted back to francs.
holdings.this_week_status: Fell about -1.8% over the past week (close 04 May 2026).

holdings.what_it_does: The world's largest food and drink company, selling more than 30 household brands — including Nescafé, KitKat, Maggi, Nespresso, and Purina pet food — across 185 countries.
why_own_it: People always need food: Nestlé sells everyday essentials that consumers buy even during recessions, making the share price generally less wild than riskier growth stocks.
Generous, reliable dividend: Nestlé has paid and grown its dividend for decades, offering an income stream of around 4% a year — above most savings accounts — while you hold the shares.
Global spread reduces risk: with sales in 185 countries and dozens of different product categories, a slowdown in one region or one brand is cushioned by the rest of the business.
holdings.one_thing_to_watch: New CEO Philipp Navratil is cutting costs and restructuring — including 16,000 job reductions — which is promising long-term but could mean short-term earnings pressure and uncertainty while the turnaround plays out.
holdings.this_week_status: Fell about -3.7% over the past week (close 04 May 2026).

sources.name: Reuters — Nestlé beats Q1 2026 sales estimates (Apr 2026)
Reuters — Nestlé cuts are appetiser for bigger shake-up (Oct 2025)

holdings.this_week_status: Fell about -1.7% over the past week (close 04 May 2026).
